eta.nationality: Approximately 59 nationalities currently visa-exempt for Schengen area
eta.notes: ETIAS (European Travel Information and Authorisation System) launching final quarter of 2026. Will apply to visa-exempt third-country nationals. Cost EUR 20, valid up to 3 years or until passport expiration, allows stays up to 90 days in any 180-day period. Switzerland participates in ETIAS as Schengen member.

visa_policy.notes: Switzerland applies Schengen Area visa rules. Maximum stay for short-stay visits is 90 days in any 180-day period. V1 = visa-free for tourism but visa required for certain work sectors (construction, catering, cleaning, security, erotic services) or any work exceeding 8 days/year. V12/V13/V14/V16/V17/V18 = conditions apply (e.g. biometric passport required). Schengen/EEA member states: no visa required. No visa-on-arrival or e-visa system exists for Switzerland. ETIAS launching final quarter of 2026 for currently visa-exempt nationalities. Source: Annex CH-1 List 1, version 3 April 2026, State Secretariat for Migration (SEM). Note: Vietnam (VNM) is NOT visa-free for ordinary passport holders — visa required; only diplomatic passport holders are exempt (see conditional_access).

credential.subtype: any valid Schengen member state residence permit

conditional_access.conditions: Third-country nationals holding both a valid residence permit issued by a Schengen member state AND a recognized valid travel document are exempt from Swiss visa requirements. Maximum stay 90 days in any 180-day period. Exception: holders of a valid residence permit issued for the purpose of transit to the issuing Schengen state are excluded. The recognized Schengen residence permits are listed in the SEM's official compilation (PDF updated 08.05.2026).

Third-country nationals holding both a valid Type D (national long-stay) visa issued by a Schengen member state AND a recognized valid travel document are exempt from Swiss visa requirements. Maximum stay 90 days in any 180-day period. Exception: holders of a valid Type D visa issued for the purpose of transit to the issuing Schengen state are excluded. Also exempts holder from work-visa obligation for certain gainful activities (long-term permit or Type D visa holders under footnotes V1, V12, V13, V14, V16, V17, V18).

A valid Schengen Type C (short-stay) visa issued by any other Schengen member state is also valid for entry into and stays in Switzerland during its period of validity, as Switzerland is a Schengen member. The visa must not be restricted to a specific Schengen state. Maximum stay subject to the 90-days-in-180-days rule across the whole Schengen Area.

cbi.notes: Switzerland does not offer a citizenship-by-investment (CBI) program. Citizenship is obtained through ordinary naturalization (minimum 10 years residence, C permit required), simplified naturalization (e.g. for spouses of Swiss citizens), or reinstatement. Dual citizenship has been fully permitted since 1 January 1992. Source: sem.admin.ch, eda.admin.ch.

programs.name: Expenditure-Based Taxation (Lump-Sum Tax) Residence
programs.notes: Foreign nationals who establish Swiss domicile for the first time (or after 10+ years abroad) and do not engage in gainful employment in Switzerland may apply for expenditure-based taxation (forfait fiscal). Tax is calculated on total annual cost of living in Switzerland and abroad. The cantonal tax ruling underpins a B-permit (residence permit) issued by cantonal authorities. Available in 21 of 26 cantons (not available in Zurich, Basel-Stadt, Basel-Landschaft, Schaffhausen, and Appenzell Ausserrhoden). No specific minimum investment is prescribed by federal law; minimum tax base is CHF 434,700 as of 2025 under federal law with cantonal minimums varying. This is not an 'investment' program per se — no minimum investment threshold is legislated federally. The residence permit type is standard B permit. Right to lump-sum taxation ends upon acquiring Swiss citizenship or gainful employment in Switzerland. Official source: Swiss Federal Department of Finance (EFD).

programs.name: EU/EFTA Residence Permit (B Permit) – Financially Independent Persons
programs.notes: EU/EFTA citizens can obtain a B permit (5-year renewable) if they can prove sufficient financial means and adequate health/accident insurance. After 5 years on B permit, eligible to apply for C permit (permanent residence). Ordinary naturalization requires 10 years total residence and C permit. Source: SEM B EU/EFTA permit page.

programs.name: Admission of Highly Qualified Workers (Non-EU/EFTA)
programs.eligibility: Managers, specialists and other qualified workers from non-EU/EFTA states. Must hold university or higher education degree with several years of professional experience. Employer must demonstrate no suitable candidate available from Switzerland or EU/EFTA (precedence principle). Annual quotas apply (set by Federal Council via ASEO ordinance).
programs.notes: Switzerland restricts non-EU/EFTA work admissions to highly qualified individuals. The employer applies for a B permit. Specific fast-track or talent visa for tech/startup not established on official SEM pages as of June 2026. Exemptions exist for multinational intra-company transfers, doctoral/post-doctoral researchers, and graduates of Swiss higher education institutions. Source: SEM.

agreements: EU-Switzerland Agreement on Free Movement of Persons (bilateral)

validation_notes: 2026-06-14 re-validation against live official sources: sem.admin.ch/sem/en/home/publiservice/weisungen-kreisschreiben/visa/liste1_staatsangehoerigkeit/v.html, sem.admin.ch diplomatic visa exemption page, sem.admin.ch FAQ entry, efd.admin.ch lump-sum page.

CORRECTION 1 — Vietnam (VNM) REMOVED from visa_free: The official sem.admin.ch List-1 alphabetical page for 'V' (https://www.sem.admin.ch/sem/en/home/publiservice/weisungen-kreisschreiben/visa/liste1_staatsangehoerigkeit/v.html) explicitly states Vietnam ordinary passport holders are VISA REQUIRED for stays up to 90 days. Vietnam was wrongly listed as visa-free. The diplomatic passport exemption (since 16 Aug 2009) is already correctly captured in conditional_access and remains.

CORRECTION 2 — Algeria conditional_access entry: passport_types updated from ['diplomatic','official'] to ['diplomatic','service','official']. The live sem.admin.ch diplomatic visa exemption page lists Algeria as 'Diplomatic, Service, Official' (agreement in force 28 May 1991). 'service' passport type was missing in the prior record.

Verified: all other visa_free entries (Albania biometric V12, Bosnia V12, Georgia V18 with 3 Apr 2026 dipl/service/official note, Kosovo V12, Moldova V12, Montenegro V12, North Macedonia V12, Serbia V13, Taiwan V14, Ukraine V12, HKSAR V16, Macao V17) match footnote definitions from leg_visum.html.
Verified: ETIAS launch 'final quarter of 2026' confirmed from sem.admin.ch/sem/en/home/themen/einreise/info-einreise/voraussetzungen-nach-staat/etias.html.

Verified: all diplomatic/service/official passport agreement dates and passport types confirmed against live sem.admin.ch/sem/en/home/international-rueckkehr/ch-migrationsaussenpolitik/abkommen/visumbefreiung-dipl.html.
Verified: cbi.has_program=false confirmed — Switzerland has no CBI program.

Verified: rbi lump-sum tax program confirmed from efd.admin.ch; 5 cantons (Zurich, Basel-Stadt, Basel-Landschaft, Schaffhausen, Appenzell Ausserrhoden) have abolished it; minimum federal tax base figure (CHF 434,700) not re-confirmed from live page — retained with gap flag.
Verified: ordinary naturalization requires 10 years residence + C permit — confirmed from sem.admin.ch/sem/en/home/integration-einbuergerung/schweizer-werden/ordentlich.html.
gaps: Exact federal minimum lump-sum tax base (CHF 434,700 cited as 2025 figure) not confirmed from the live efd.admin.ch page during this validation; EFD page did not display the numeric figure explicitly. Recommend direct check of Art. 14 para. 2 DBG (Swiss Federal Direct Tax Act).
Processing times for B permit (highly qualified worker) not published verbatim on official SEM English pages.
ETIAS exact launch date within Q4 2026 not yet finalized; EU will announce several months in advance.
No visa-on-arrival or e-visa system exists for Switzerland — these categories are empty by official confirmation, not by omission.
Fast-track or dedicated startup/digital-nomad visa: no official SEM program found as of June 2026.
No OCI (India Overseas Citizen of India) card exemption found for Switzerland — OCI is an Indian domestic benefit granting entry to India, not to Switzerland.

No credential-based exemption found for holders of US, UK, Canadian, Australian, or other non-Schengen visas — only Schengen member state residence permits and Type C/D visas trigger Swiss visa-free entry.
The specific list of which Schengen member state residence permits are recognized is a PDF updated 08.05.2026 at sem.admin.ch; individual permit types per country not enumerated here.

Vietnam ordinary passport holders (VNM): visa REQUIRED — removed from visa_free in this validation. Not placed in any alternative access category as no visa-on-arrival or e-visa exists for Switzerland.

outbound_official.note: No single official Swiss government origin-side page lists where Swiss ordinary passport holders can travel without a prior consular visa. The EDA/FDFA (eda.admin.ch) provides per-country entry-and-stay dossiers for Swiss citizens at eda.admin.ch/eda/en/fdfa/living-abroad/auslandaufenthalt/auswandern/laenderinformationen.html (an alphabetical country index), and a travel hub at eda.admin.ch/en/travel, but neither page constitutes a consolidated visa-status list comparable to India's MEA VFFIN page. The Swiss government's Travel Admin app (available via eda.admin.ch/eda/en/fdfa/fdfa/aktuell/newsuebersicht/2021/06/travel-admin.html) provides trip-specific outbound visa information per destination but is an app, not a structured web list. Rely on destination-side data for outbound access analysis.

sources_checked.description: Annex CH-1 List 1 – Overview of ID and visa provisions according to nationality, version 3 April 2026, State Secretariat for Migration (SEM)
SEM FAQ – Entry (ETIAS, Schengen rules, Q2.10 Schengen residence permit holders, Q2.11 Type C visa validity)
SEM – Visa legislation footnotes; footnote V definition (Schengen residence permit / Type D visa exemption); footnotes V1, V12–V18
SEM – Visa exemption agreements for holders of diplomatic, service, special and official passports (full bilateral agreement list with entry-into-force dates)
SEM – Visa waiver agreements for standard/ordinary passports
SEM – Entry overview (confirms Schengen Type C visa valid for Switzerland as Schengen member)
SEM – Ordinary naturalisation (10 years residence, C permit, no investment pathway)
SEM – How to become a Swiss citizen (naturalization overview)
SEM – B EU/EFTA Permit (resident foreign nationals, 5-year renewable)
SEM – Basis for admission to Swiss employment market for non-EU/EFTA nationals
Swiss Federal Department of Finance (EFD) – Expenditure-based (lump-sum) taxation
